title: SCPN Quantum Control — Differentiable Phase-QNode Claim Ledger

claims.claim_text: Optional JAX, PyTorch, TensorFlow CPU, and PennyLane parity is reproducible from an explicit overlay profile.
known_gaps: Local machines must still run the manifest install command before parity can execute.
The overlay is CPU-only and intentionally excludes the repository jax[cuda12] extra.
claims.claim_boundary: Framework overlay evidence is installation reproducibility only; it is not GPU, provider, QPU, or performance evidence.

claims.claim_text: Differentiable Phase-QNode benchmark artefacts carry CI runner, isolation, load, governor, and artefact-ID metadata.
known_gaps: GitHub-hosted runners are downgraded to functional_non_isolated.
Production isolated_affinity promotion requires a self-hosted isolated-benchmark runner artefact.
Accelerator benchmark claims require explicit CUDA/ROCm device metadata; missing visible devices are classified as silent_accelerator_fallback.
claims.claim_boundary: Non-isolated artefacts support functional parity and regression tracking only, not production speed claims.

claims.claim_text: The bounded Phase-QNode objective has explicit comparison rows for JAX, PyTorch, TensorFlow, PennyLane, and optional Enzyme.
known_gaps: Missing framework dependencies are hard_gap rows.
Unconfigured LLVM/Enzyme tooling remains dependency_missing; configured runners must pass strict JSON, timeout, toolchain, and correctness gates.
claims.claim_boundary: External comparison rows are bounded CPU diagnostic evidence only; they do not claim provider or QPU execution.

claims.claim_text: Phase-QNode public wording remains bounded to artefact-backed differentiable execution and does not promote broad framework or hardware claims.
known_gaps: The lane remains SOTA-candidate until isolated CI benchmark and external comparison artefacts pass.
Provider and QPU execution remain explicitly outside this claim.
claims.claim_boundary: Bounded Phase-QNode differentiable execution only; no broad quantum advantage, provider, QPU, or production performance claim.

claims.claim_text: Differentiable claim-ledger rows are checked against existing implementation, test, docs, and generated capability-manifest surfaces.
known_gaps: The audit checks committed generated-manifest inventory and does not replace isolated benchmark artefacts.
It validates support-surface wiring, not provider hardware execution or production performance.
claims.claim_boundary: Support-surface alignment only; verifies implementation, tests, docs, and generated inventory without promoting performance, provider, or QPU claims.

claims.claim_text: Every differentiable hardening slice has a typed checklist for focused Ruff, mypy, module-specific pytest, test-quality audit, claim-ledger validation, and benchmark-evidence classification checks.
known_gaps: The gate builds an auditable verification checklist but does not execute shell commands.
It checks benchmark-classification invariants and does not upgrade functional_non_isolated rows to isolated_affinity evidence.
claims.claim_boundary: Per-slice hardening governance only; no provider execution, QPU execution, accelerator execution, or production benchmark promotion is implied.

claims.claim_text: Differentiable-programming modules are audited against the live module inventory, module-specific test surfaces, and declared fail-closed diagnostic surfaces before promotion.
known_gaps: The audit verifies inventory, tests, and declared diagnostics; it does not prove full formal correctness.
Provider execution, hardware execution, and isolated benchmark promotion remain separate evidence gates.
claims.claim_boundary: Module-hardening governance only; no provider execution, QPU execution, full formal proof, or production benchmark promotion is implied.

claims.claim_text: The differentiable external-validation package has exact environment lockfile checksums for runtime, development, CI, framework overlay, and Enzyme runner reproduction.
known_gaps: The lock manifest records reproducibility inputs only and does not execute external validation jobs.
The artefact remains functional_non_isolated and cannot promote benchmark or hardware claims.
claims.claim_boundary: External-validation environment lockfiles support reviewer reproduction only; they do not promote performance, provider, QPU, GPU, hardware, or isolated_affinity benchmark claims.

claims.claim_text: The differentiable lane exposes a public-safe claim table generated from the committed ledger.
known_gaps: The table constrains wording but does not execute external validation or isolated benchmarks.
Rows remain bounded candidates unless the claim ledger promotes them with passing evidence.
claims.claim_boundary: Public claim table governance only; no provider execution, QPU execution, accelerator execution, hardware execution, or production benchmark promotion is implied.

claims.claim_text: The differentiable external-validation package has a reproducible manifest over committed evidence artefacts.
known_gaps: The manifest records committed evidence checksums but does not bundle private or ignored artefacts.
The artefact remains functional_non_isolated and cannot promote benchmark or hardware claims.
claims.claim_boundary: External-validation artefact-bundle governance only; no provider execution, QPU execution, accelerator execution, hardware execution, or production benchmark promotion is implied.

claims.claim_text: The differentiable lane has a public technical report summarizing comparison evidence, reproducibility artefacts, provider-family status, and promotion blockers.
known_gaps: The report is descriptive and does not execute external validation jobs or isolated benchmarks.
Rows remain bounded candidates unless the claim ledger promotes them with passing evidence.
claims.claim_boundary: Technical-report governance only; no provider execution, QPU execution, accelerator execution, hardware execution, or production benchmark promotion is implied.
